prompt: Given the empathy reality (a software company shipped AI in their PRODUCT/PLATFORM, and enterprise deals now stall in security review over that AI; the buyer identifies with their product, not 'an agent'; they want to pass the review / answer the AI security questionnaire / prove the AI is safe), write 8 RELATABLE, off-the-shelf homepage headline candidates that a product builder instantly recognizes as their problem. Constraints: security-led, plain and human (not jargon, not cryptographic-first, not 'agent'-narrow unless truly apt), no em/en dashes, do not overclaim that the AI is 'safe/secure/certified'. Anchor on the product/AI-feature the buyer identifies with and the security-review/questionnaire pain. For each headline give the 1-line subhead and why a real buyer relates. Then rank them and name the single best plus 2 alternates.
content:
**1. Headline:** Enterprise deals stall once the AI lands in your product  
**Subhead:** Security teams flag the feature buyers already rely on.  
**Why a real buyer relates:** They shipped the AI update to differentiate the product, then watched procurement cycles extend by weeks or months over the same capability.

**2. Headline:** Security questionnaires now target the AI inside your platform  
**Subhead:** Every new enterprise buyer sends the same set of questions about the feature they want.  
**Why a real buyer relates:** The product team sees the AI as a natural extension of existing functionality, while reviewers treat it as an entirely new category of risk.

**3. Headline:** Your product’s AI capability keeps triggering extra security reviews  
**Subhead:** Deals that used to close cleanly now require new questionnaire responses.  
**Why a real buyer relates:** Sales teams report the exact same friction point across multiple accounts after the AI shipped.

**4. Headline:** Buyers love the AI in your product until security gets involved  
**Subhead:** The feature that wins evaluations becomes the item that holds up final approval.  
**Why a real buyer relates:** Product and sales see clear user value, yet the internal security process creates an unexpected bottleneck.

**5. Headline:** The AI you added to differentiate now requires security questionnaire answers  
**Subhead:** Responses must map back to how the feature actually works in the product.  
**Why a real buyer relates:** They need practical language that matches the product description they already give customers, not abstract model details.

**6. Headline:** Security review cycles lengthen because of the AI feature in your platform  
**Subhead:** The same questions appear in every new enterprise evaluation.  
**Why a real buyer relates:** Teams recognize the repeating pattern and want materials that address it without restarting from scratch each time.

**7. Headline:** Your shipped AI capability is now the focus of customer security reviews  
**Subhead:** Answer questions while keeping the conversation anchored on the product feature.  
**Why a real buyer relates:** The buyer already understands and values the feature; the questionnaire just needs to align with that view.

**8. Headline:** AI in the product means new security questions at every enterprise deal stage  
**Subhead:** Prepare responses that fit the feature buyers already evaluated.  
**Why a real buyer relates:** They watch the same friction appear late in the sales process and want to shorten or eliminate it.

**Ranking and selection**  
Best: #2 (directly names the questionnaire pain while anchoring on the product/platform buyers already know).  
Strong alternates: #1 and #3 (both cleanly capture the deal-stall reality without narrowing to “agent” language or promising safety).
